hooks.prompt:
Check the edited code for PostgreSQL best practices. Flag if you see ANY of these issues:
1. Using VARCHAR(n) instead of TEXT (no performance benefit in PostgreSQL)
2. Using TIMESTAMP instead of TIMESTAMPTZ (always store with timezone)
3. Using FLOAT/REAL for money (use NUMERIC)
4. Using JSON instead of JSONB (JSONB is faster and indexable)
5. String concatenation in SQL queries instead of parameterized queries ($1, $2)
6. CREATE INDEX without CONCURRENTLY (blocks writes on large tables)
7. Using sequential/integer IDs in public APIs (use UUID for public-facing)
8. Missing indexes on foreign key columns
9. Using ENUM types (hard to modify — use CHECK constraints or lookup tables)
10. VACUUM FULL in production code (blocks all access — use regular VACUUM)
11. work_mem set too high globally (it's per-sort, not per-query)
12. Missing ON DELETE clause on foreign keys (default RESTRICT may not be intended)

Only flag clear violations. Respond with {"ok": true} if the code looks fine, or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issue"} if there's a problem.
